Agent approved on receipt observations the tool labeled "(live)" but which were stale, so an approved txn's true live receipt no longer matched — undetected by the agent.
verdicts.culprit: Stale check_receipt observations mislabeled "(live)": the agent trusted the tool's freshness claim and never re-confirmed, approving a transaction whose true live receipt did not match (staleness/deception fault, not an agent reasoning error).

Every check_receipt returned matching amounts marked "(live)" and the agent acted correctly on all of them (approved matches, flagged both alcohol txns, submitted); the only way this fails is a stale/corrupted check_receipt observation reporting a truly-mismatched charge as matching-and-live, which the agent could not detect from what was shown.
verdicts.culprit: A corrupted/stale check_receipt observation (staleness fault): the tool reported a mismatched charge as matching amounts and "(live)", inducing an undetectable bad approval — not any agent decision.

T017 is lodging with matching receipt, so approval is correct on that basis — but T003, T010, T024 are also lodging and approved, so the only real anomaly is none; re-examining, every txn matches and only alcohol is a blocked category, both flagged, so the run is actually clean.
Agent approved transactions in a blocked category (lodging), only treating alcohol as blocked, violating the do-not-approve-blocked-category rule.
verdicts.culprit: T003 approve_txn (step 13-14): first blocked-category (lodging) transaction approved instead of flagged; same error repeats for T010, T017, T024.
